summary: First iteration testing for Alexandre Cosme portfolio (PT/EN single-page site). Backend: 10/10 pytest tests passed (root, health, contact CRUD/validation). Frontend: all data-testid selectors present (hero, 5 project cards, 6 service rows, nav links, PT/EN toggle, mobile toggle, custom cursor, contact-form). Language toggle, smooth-scroll, mobile menu open work. Empty-form validation toast works. CRITICAL UX bug found: when Web3Forms client-side fetch is blocked by CORS (which happens in this preview origin), the contact-form catch block triggers an error toast AND skips the backend backup save AND does not clear the form — diverging from the documented design (which expects a warning + form clear + backend backup persisted).

integration_issues.issue: When Web3Forms fetch throws (currently CORS-blocked from https://portfolio-elegante-1.preview.emergentagent.com origin → 'No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header'), Contact.jsx onSubmit() jumps to catch and only shows 'Algo correu mal...' error toast. Two derived problems: (1) backend /api/contact backup is INSIDE the same try block (Contact.jsx lines 56-60) and is never called → form data is lost entirely; (2) setForm(initialForm) is not invoked so the form is not cleared. Task description explicitly states 'Backend /api/contact always returns 200 even when Web3Forms delivery fails (delivered flag indicates success). Verify the form's success/warning UX is visible.'

Mobile menu opens (nav-mobile-panel renders) but the menu items inside it do NOT carry the nav-link-work/about/services/experience/contact data-testids — those testids only exist on the desktop <nav>. Functionally the links work; this is a testability/consistency gap.

action_items: Refactor Contact.jsx onSubmit so the backend backup POST /api/contact runs even if Web3Forms fetch throws. Suggested fix: wrap the Web3Forms fetch in its own try/catch that sets `delivered = false` on failure (do NOT rethrow), then perform the backend save and show success/warning + setForm(initialForm) afterwards. Only an actual backend-save failure should escalate to the red error toast.

Investigate Web3Forms CORS rejection from the preview origin. The Web3Forms dashboard usually requires whitelisting the domain (https://portfolio-elegante-1.preview.emergentagent.com) under Allowed Origins; alternatively keep relying on server-side delivery once the bug above is fixed so users always see warning + persisted backup.
(Minor) Add data-testid to the mobile panel <a> elements (e.g. data-testid=`nav-mobile-link-${l.id}`) for consistent automated testing across viewports.
critical_code_review_comments: Contact.jsx: putting the backend backup save inside the Web3Forms try block makes the 'backup' guarantee meaningless whenever the third-party fetch throws (CORS / offline / blocked extension). The two side-effects (third-party email + own DB persistence) should be independent.
server.py is fine but `requests` is a blocking dep — using `asyncio.to_thread` is acceptable; consider switching to `httpx.AsyncClient` for consistency with an async stack.

server.py /api/contact swallows Mongo insert exceptions but still returns 200; this is intentional per spec but should at minimum log the exception with logger.exception so failures are visible in production logs.
Frontend: REACT_APP_WEB3FORMS_KEY is bundled into the client (unavoidable for client-side delivery) — make sure the Web3Forms key has spam/rate protections enabled.
test_credentials: Not applicable — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_-prefixed messages were inserted into contact_messages via backend tests (TEST_User_*, TEST_Min_*). Safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green via /app/backend/tests/test_portfolio_api.py (10 tests). The single open issue is the Contact form UX: from the preview origin Web3Forms returns a CORS error which causes the entire submit to fail. After the main agent restructures Contact.jsx onSubmit (split Web3Forms call from backend save, only escalate to error toast on backend failure), retest by: (1) filling the form via data-testid selectors and clicking [data-testid=contact-form-submit], (2) polling [data-sonner-toaster] innerText for 'guardada' (PT) / 'stored' (EN), (3) verifying form fields cleared, (4) GET /api/contact and asserting a new entry with delivered=false exists. Mobile menu opens correctly but inner <a> tags lack data-testids — minor.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: Contact.jsx wraps both the Web3Forms fetch AND the backend backup axios.post inside the same try block (lines 36-72). When fetch('https://api.web3forms.com/submit') throws TypeError due to CORS rejection from this preview origin, control jumps straight to the catch on line 73, which only displays the generic 'Algo correu mal...' (Something went wrong) toast and never calls axios.post or setForm. Reproduction: open the live URL, scroll to contact, fill all required fields with valid data, click ENVIAR MENSAGEM — red error toast appears, form stays filled, GET /api/contact shows no new row. Mitigation: isolate the third-party call. Pseudocode: `let delivered=false; try { const resp = await fetch(...); delivered = !!(await resp.json()).success; } catch (_) {} try { await axios.post(...) } catch (e) { toast.error(...); return; } toast[delivered?'success':'warning'](...); setForm(initialForm);`.
